questions.question: What does the sandbox attribute on an <iframe> do?
choices: It sets the background color of the iframe
It applies extra restrictions on the content within the iframe
It makes the iframe resizable by the user
It enables offline caching for the iframe content
questions.explanation: The sandbox attribute enables a set of restrictions on iframe content, such as blocking scripts, form submissions, and popups. Specific restrictions can be relaxed by adding values like allow-scripts.

questions.question: Which attribute allows you to embed HTML content directly within an <iframe> without loading an external URL?
questions.explanation: The srcdoc attribute contains inline HTML that the iframe renders directly. When both src and srcdoc are present, srcdoc takes priority.

questions.question: Which sandbox value must be added to allow JavaScript execution inside a sandboxed iframe?
questions.explanation: By default, a sandboxed iframe blocks all scripts. Adding allow-scripts to the sandbox attribute re-enables JavaScript execution within the iframe.

questions.question: What is a key security concern when using <iframe> elements?
choices: Iframes increase page load speed too much
Iframes can be used for clickjacking attacks by overlaying invisible frames
Iframes automatically share cookies with the parent page
questions.explanation: Clickjacking is a major iframe security concern where a malicious site overlays an invisible iframe over trusted content, tricking users into clicking on something they did not intend. The X-Frame-Options header and sandbox attribute help mitigate this.

questions.question: The ___ attribute on an <iframe> specifies the URL of the page to embed.
questions.explanation: The src attribute provides the URL of the external document to display within the iframe.

questions.question: To allow form submission inside a sandboxed iframe, you must add the ___ value to the sandbox attribute.
questions.explanation: The allow-forms token re-enables form submission within a sandboxed iframe. Without it, any form submissions inside the iframe are blocked.
